common.failedLoadUsers: Failed to load users. Make sure the API is reachable.

docs.browseDescription: Browse all available UI components. Each page includes live previews, usage examples, and API references.
docs.extendsAllProps: Extends all props from the Button primitive plus the following:
docs.recommendedForAppDesc: Use this import for all pages, features, and modules. The raw Button primitive should only be used when building new reusable atoms or molecules.
docs.designSystemPrimitiveDesc: Do not modify this file directly — it is the design token layer generated by shadcn. For application code (pages, features, modules), use the Button Atom which adds loading state and ref forwarding.
docs.reexportNote: — a re-export for consistency with the atomic design import pattern. No additional functionality.
docs.useSizeProp: Use the size prop to change the size.
docs.useVariantProp: Use the variant prop to change the style.

button.desc: Displays a button or a component that looks like a button. This is the raw shadcn/ui primitive built on @base-ui/react/button with CVA-driven styling.
button.whenToUse: Use this primitive directly only when building new reusable atoms or molecules inside src/components/. For all application-level buttons, import from @/components/atoms/Button instead.

buttonAtom.desc: Application-level button wrapper that extends the Button primitive with a loading spinner, automatic disable-on-load, and ref forwarding.
buttonAtom.whenToUse: Use this for every button in application code — form submits, action triggers, navigation. It wraps the shadcn primitive with application-level behavior so you don't need to manually wire up loading spinners or disabled states during async operations.
buttonAtom.loadingDesc: The loading prop renders a Spinner and automatically disables the button.
buttonAtom.asyncDesc: Typical usage with React state to toggle loading during an async operation.

avatar.desc: An image element with a fallback for representing the user.
avatar.whenToUse: Use for displaying user profile images, team member lists, or any entity that needs a visual representation with a text fallback. Combine with AvatarGroup for stacked displays. Pair with DropdownMenu for user menus.
avatar.badgeDesc: Use AvatarBadge to add a status indicator.
avatar.fallbackDesc: The fallback is displayed when the image fails to load or no src is provided.

badge.desc: Displays a badge or a component that looks like a badge.
badge.whenToUse: Use for status indicators, labels, counts, or tags. Common in table cells (e.g., user roles, payment status), navigation items, and card headers. Pick the variant that matches the semantic meaning — destructive for errors, secondary for neutral labels.

card.desc: Displays a card with header, content, and footer.
card.whenToUse: Use for grouping related content into a visually distinct container with consistent spacing. Ideal for dashboard widgets, settings panels, and content sections. For metric displays, use the StatCard molecule which builds on Card.

input.desc: A text input component for forms and user data entry with built-in styling and accessibility features.
input.whenToUse: Use for single-line text input in forms. For a complete form field with label and error handling, use the FormField molecule instead. For multi-line text, use a native textarea styled with Tailwind.

label.desc: Renders an accessible label associated with controls.
label.whenToUse: Use to associate accessible labels with form controls. For most cases, use the FormField molecule which already includes Label. Use Label directly when building custom form layouts.

separator.whenToUse: Use to visually divide sections of content. Horizontal separators work between stacked content blocks. Vertical separators work between inline items like navigation links or toolbar buttons.

sheet.desc: Extends the Dialog component to display content that complements the main content of the screen.
sheet.whenToUse: Use for secondary content that complements the main view — filters, settings panels, detail views, or mobile navigation. Prefer Sheet over Dialog when the content is supplementary and doesn't require a decision before continuing.

table.whenToUse: Use for displaying structured tabular data. For data that needs pagination, sorting, or column management, use the DataTable organism which builds on these primitives with TanStack Table. Use the raw Table for simple, static data displays.

tooltip.desc: A popup that displays information related to an element when the element receives keyboard focus or the mouse hovers over it.
tooltip.whenToUse: Use to provide additional context for UI elements on hover/focus. Ideal for icon-only buttons, truncated text, or controls that need a brief explanation. Keep tooltip text short — for longer content, use Popover instead.
tooltip.positionsDesc: Use the side prop to change the tooltip position.

dropdownMenu.desc: Displays a menu to the user — such as a set of actions or functions — triggered by a button.
dropdownMenu.whenToUse: Use for contextual actions triggered by a button — user menus, row actions in tables, settings dropdowns. For navigation menus, use NavigationMenu instead. For command palettes, use Command.

spinner.desc: An indicator that can be used to show a loading state.
spinner.whenToUse: Use as a standalone loading indicator for sections or full-page loading states. For buttons with loading states, use the Button Atom which integrates Spinner automatically.
spinner.sizesDesc: Use the size prop to change the spinner size.

skeleton.desc: Use to show a placeholder while content is loading.
skeleton.whenToUse: Use as placeholder loading states that match the shape of the content being loaded. Compose multiple skeletons to build loading layouts for cards, lists, and profiles. Prefer Skeleton over Spinner when you want to preserve the layout structure during loading.
skeleton.cardSkeletonDesc: Compose multiple skeletons to create placeholder layouts.

formField.desc: Combines Label, Input, and error text into a single accessible form field.
formField.whenToUse: Use for standard form inputs that need a label and error handling. Combines Label, Input, and error text with proper aria attributes automatically. For custom inputs (selects, date pickers), compose your own field using Label + Input directly.
formField.errorDesc: Pass the error prop to show validation errors.
formField.nativePropsNote: Extends all native <input> props plus the following:

statCard.desc: A metric card for displaying key statistics with a title, value, description, and optional icon.
statCard.whenToUse: Use for displaying key metrics on dashboards — revenue, user counts, growth rates. Builds on the Card primitive with a standardized layout for title, value, description, and icon. For custom card layouts, use the Card primitive directly.

themeToggle.desc: A button that toggles between light and dark themes using next-themes.
themeToggle.whenToUse: Use in navigation bars to let users switch between light and dark themes. Requires the ThemeProvider from next-themes to be set up in the root layout (already configured in this boilerplate).
themeToggle.depDesc: Requires next-themes ThemeProvider to be set up in the root layout. This component has no props — it reads and writes the theme via the useTheme hook.

localeSwitcher.desc: A pair of toggle buttons for switching between Indonesian (ID) and English (EN) locales.
localeSwitcher.whenToUse: Use in navigation bars to let users switch between Indonesian (ID) and English (EN) locales. The active locale is managed by the Zustand UI store and consumed by the i18n translation system.
localeSwitcher.depDesc: Reads and writes the locale via the useUIStore Zustand store. This component has no props.

pageHeader.desc: A page-level header with title, optional description, and action buttons.
pageHeader.whenToUse: Use at the top of every page to provide a consistent title, optional description, and action buttons. This is the standard page header pattern used across the boilerplate — see the Dashboard and Users pages for real examples.

dataTable.desc: Powerful table and datagrids built using TanStack Table with built-in pagination.
dataTable.whenToUse: Use for tabular data that needs client-side pagination. Define columns using TanStack Table's ColumnDef and pass data as an array. For simple read-only tables without pagination, use the base Table primitive directly.
dataTable.defaultDesc: Define columns using TanStack Table's ColumnDef and pass data as an array.
dataTable.pageSizeDesc: Use pageSize to control rows per page.

checkbox.desc: A control that allows the user to toggle between checked and not checked.
checkbox.whenToUse: Use for boolean form inputs — terms acceptance, feature toggles, multi-select lists. For mutually exclusive options, use Radio Group instead.

selectComp.desc: Displays a list of options for the user to pick from — triggered by a button.
selectComp.whenToUse: Use when the user must pick one option from a list of 5+. For fewer options, consider Radio Group. For native behavior on mobile, use Native Select.

switchComp.desc: A control that allows the user to toggle between checked and not checked.
switchComp.whenToUse: Use for instant on/off toggles — enable notifications, dark mode, feature flags. Unlike Checkbox, Switch implies the change takes effect immediately.

radioGroup.desc: A set of checkable buttons where no more than one can be checked at a time.
radioGroup.whenToUse: Use when the user must pick exactly one option from a small set (2-5 options). For longer lists, use Select instead.

textareaComp.desc: Displays a form textarea for multi-line text input.
textareaComp.whenToUse: Use for multi-line text input — comments, descriptions, messages. For single-line text, use Input instead.

sliderComp.desc: An input where the user selects a value from within a given range.
sliderComp.whenToUse: Use for numeric range inputs — volume, brightness, price range. For precise numeric input, use Input with type='number' instead.

nativeSelect.desc: A styled native HTML select element with consistent design system integration.
nativeSelect.whenToUse: Use when you need native OS select behavior, especially on mobile devices. For custom-styled dropdowns, use the Select component.

fieldComp.desc: Combine labels, controls, and help text to compose accessible form fields.
fieldComp.whenToUse: Use as the building block for form fields. Provides proper label association, description text, and error messages with correct ARIA attributes.

inputGroup.desc: Add addons, buttons, and helper content to inputs.
inputGroup.whenToUse: Use to add visual context to inputs — currency symbols, URL prefixes, action buttons. Wraps Input with prefix/suffix elements.

inputOtp.desc: Accessible one-time password component with copy-paste functionality.
inputOtp.whenToUse: Use for verification code inputs — email OTP, 2FA codes, phone verification. Supports paste from clipboard and auto-advance.

dialog.desc: A window overlaid on the primary window, rendering the content underneath inert.
dialog.whenToUse: Use for focused interactions that require user attention — forms, confirmations, detail views. For non-blocking supplementary content, use Sheet instead.

alertDialog.desc: A modal dialog that interrupts the user with important content and expects a response.
alertDialog.whenToUse: Use for destructive or irreversible actions that require explicit confirmation — delete, discard changes, logout. For general dialogs, use Dialog instead.

popoverComp.desc: Displays rich content in a portal, triggered by a button.
popoverComp.whenToUse: Use for contextual content that needs more space than a Tooltip — settings panels, color pickers, filter forms. For simple text hints, use Tooltip.

hoverCard.desc: For sighted users to preview content available behind a link.
hoverCard.whenToUse: Use for link previews — user profiles, article summaries, repository info. Content appears on hover and should be supplementary, not essential.

sonnerComp.desc: An opinionated toast component for temporary notifications.
sonnerComp.whenToUse: Use for brief, non-blocking feedback — success messages, error alerts, status updates. Toasts auto-dismiss and stack at the bottom of the viewport.

progressComp.desc: Displays an indicator showing the completion progress of a task.
progressComp.whenToUse: Use for determinate progress — file uploads, form steps, data processing. For indeterminate loading, use Spinner instead.

emptyComp.desc: Display an empty state when there is no data to show.
emptyComp.whenToUse: Use when a list, table, or section has no content — first-time users, empty search results, filtered-out data. Provides a consistent empty state pattern.

drawerComp.desc: A drawer component that slides in from the edge of the screen.
drawerComp.whenToUse: Use for mobile-friendly panels — navigation, filters, quick actions. Slides from bottom by default. For side panels, use Sheet instead.

tabs.desc: A set of layered sections of content displayed one at a time.
tabs.whenToUse: Use to organize related content into switchable views — settings categories, data views, form steps. Keeps all content in the DOM for fast switching.

accordionComp.desc: A vertically stacked set of interactive headings that each reveal a section of content.
accordionComp.whenToUse: Use for collapsible content sections — FAQs, settings groups, documentation. Saves vertical space while keeping content accessible.

collapsibleComp.desc: An interactive component which expands/collapses a panel.
collapsibleComp.whenToUse: Use for single expandable sections. For multiple collapsible sections, use Accordion instead. Ideal for 'show more' patterns.

breadcrumbComp.desc: Displays the path to the current resource using a hierarchy of links.
breadcrumbComp.whenToUse: Use for hierarchical navigation — nested pages, category trees, multi-step flows. Helps users understand their location and navigate back.

paginationComp.desc: Pagination with page navigation, next and previous links.
paginationComp.whenToUse: Use for paginated content — search results, data lists, article archives. For table pagination, the DataTable organism has this built in.

navigationMenu.desc: A collection of links for navigating websites.
navigationMenu.whenToUse: Use for top-level site navigation with dropdown mega-menus. For simpler menus, use DropdownMenu. For app navigation, use Sidebar.

menubarComp.desc: A visually persistent menu common in desktop applications.
menubarComp.whenToUse: Use for desktop-style menu bars — File/Edit/View menus, toolbar menus. For single dropdown menus, use DropdownMenu instead.

scrollArea.desc: Augments native scroll functionality for custom, cross-browser styling.
scrollArea.whenToUse: Use when you need styled scrollbars that look consistent across browsers. Wraps content with custom scrollbar tracks and thumbs.

resizableComp.desc: Accessible resizable panel groups and layouts with keyboard support.
resizableComp.whenToUse: Use for adjustable layouts — code editors with preview, split views, configurable dashboards. Supports horizontal and vertical splitting.

sidebarComp.desc: A composable, themeable and customizable sidebar component.
sidebarComp.whenToUse: Use for application navigation sidebars. This is the shadcn sidebar primitive — the boilerplate already has a custom Sidebar organism built on simpler patterns.

commandComp.desc: Command menu for search and quick actions.
commandComp.whenToUse: Use for command palettes (Cmd+K / Ctrl+K) — quick navigation, search, action execution. Built on cmdk for keyboard-first interaction.

comboboxComp.desc: Autocomplete input with a list of suggestions.
comboboxComp.whenToUse: Use for searchable select inputs — user lookup, tag selection, location search. Combines Input with a filterable dropdown list.

contextMenu.desc: Displays a menu of actions triggered by a right click.
contextMenu.whenToUse: Use for right-click context menus — file actions, table row actions, canvas tools. For button-triggered menus, use DropdownMenu.

calendarComp.desc: A calendar component for date selection.
calendarComp.whenToUse: Use for standalone date picking — event scheduling, date range selection. For inline date inputs in forms, combine with Popover for a Date Picker pattern.

carouselComp.desc: A carousel with motion and swipe built using Embla.
carouselComp.whenToUse: Use for horizontally scrollable content — image galleries, testimonials, feature showcases. Supports autoplay, looping, and touch/swipe gestures.

chartComp.whenToUse: Use for data visualization — bar charts, line charts, area charts, pie charts. Provides a themed chart container that integrates with the design system colors.

toggleComp.desc: A two-state button that can be either on or off.
toggleComp.whenToUse: Use for togglable actions — bold/italic in editors, view mode switches, filter toggles. For on/off settings, prefer Switch.

toggleGroupComp.desc: A set of two-state buttons that can be toggled on or off.
toggleGroupComp.whenToUse: Use for grouped toggle options — text alignment, view modes, filter sets. Supports single or multiple selection.

buttonGroupComp.desc: A container that groups related buttons together with consistent styling.
buttonGroupComp.whenToUse: Use to visually group related actions — toolbar buttons, segmented controls, action sets. Provides connected border styling.

kbdComp.desc: Used to display textual user input from keyboard.
kbdComp.whenToUse: Use to display keyboard shortcuts — in tooltips, menus, documentation. Shows styled key representations like Ctrl, Shift, Enter.

alertComp.desc: Displays a callout for user attention.
alertComp.whenToUse: Use for persistent inline messages — warnings, info notices, success confirmations. For temporary notifications, use Sonner (toast).

aspectRatio.desc: Displays content within a desired ratio.
aspectRatio.whenToUse: Use to maintain consistent aspect ratios for media — images, videos, maps. Prevents layout shift during loading.

itemComp.desc: A versatile component for displaying content with media, title, description, and actions.
itemComp.whenToUse: Use for list items, menu entries, or any content that combines an icon/image with text and optional actions.

directionComp.desc: A provider component that sets the text direction for your application.
directionComp.whenToUse: Use to support RTL (right-to-left) layouts for Arabic, Hebrew, and other RTL languages. Wraps content with the correct dir attribute.

typographyComp.desc: Styles for headings, paragraphs, lists, and other text elements.
typographyComp.whenToUse: Reference for consistent typography across the application. Use Tailwind classes to apply heading, body, and prose styles.
